title: trifolium kingii subsp. productum
intro: Trifolium kingii subsp. productum, commonly known as King's clover, is a subspecies of the clover plant, which is a type of legume that belongs to the family Fabaceae. It is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The plant is rich in vitamins and minerals, and its consumption is believed to offer various health benefits, such as improving digestion, reducing stress, and boosting the immune system. In herbal medicine, King's clover is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being added to salads and used as a natural dye.
